title: Income Tax Ordinance, 2001 - Basic Concepts and Definitions
description: 50 MCQs covering definitions of tax, taxpayer, person, company, heads of income, tax year, principal officer, types of companies, and resident/non-resident status based on Part 1 and Part 2 lectures.

questions.text: Under Section 2(63) of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001, the term 'Tax' includes which of the following?
options: Income tax, default surcharge, penalty, and fee

questions.text: What is the rate of default surcharge payable on the amount of tax if it is not paid on time?
Who is defined as a 'Taxpayer' under Section 2(61) of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001?
options: Any person who is liable to pay tax

questions.text: Which of the following is included in the definition of 'Person' under the Income Tax Ordinance?
For tax purposes, a Provincial Government is treated as:
Which of the following entities is included in the definition of 'Company' under Section 2(10)?
options: Only companies registered under Companies Ordinance

questions.text: A partnership firm registered under the Partnership Act, 1932 is treated as:
A Hindu Undivided Family (HUF) falls under which category for income tax purposes?
How many heads of income are defined under the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001?
Income from Salary is chargeable to tax under which section?
Income from Property is chargeable to tax under which section?
Income from Business is chargeable to tax under which section?
Income from Capital Gains is chargeable to tax under which section?
Income from Other Sources is chargeable to tax under which section?
Income earned or received from within Pakistan is classified as:
Income received from sources outside Pakistan is classified as:
'Total Income' under the Ordinance means:
options: Aggregate of income from all heads of income

questions.text: Taxable Income is computed as:
options: Total Income minus deductions and allowances

questions.text: Normal Tax Year in Pakistan covers which period?
Tax Year 2020 under the normal scheme refers to:
Special Tax Year is granted to a taxpayer by:
options: Federal Board of Revenue on request

questions.text: Which industry in Pakistan commonly uses a Special Tax Year from 1st October to 30th September?
Transitional Tax Year applies when:
options: A company changes from Normal to Special Tax Year

questions.text: During a Transitional Tax Year, a taxpayer is required to:
options: File a separate tax return for the gap period

questions.text: Why is a Principal Officer required for a company or AOP?
options: Because they are artificial persons and cannot act themselves

questions.text: Who among the following can act as a Principal Officer of a company?
options: Director, Manager, Secretary, Accountant, or any designated officer

questions.text: The Commissioner of Income Tax has the power to:
options: Only accept returns from the CEO
Declare any person connected with management as Principal Officer

questions.text: A Public Company includes a company in which the Federal or Provincial Government holds at least:
A company whose shares are listed and traded on the Pakistan Stock Exchange is treated as:
A company whose shares are widely available to the public is classified as:
A Private Company is defined under the Ordinance as:
options: A company with less than 10 members
A company that is not a public company

questions.text: Which of the following is a criterion for a Small Company regarding capital?
options: Paid-up capital + reserves exceeding Rs. 100 million
Paid-up capital + reserves not exceeding Rs. 50 million
Paid-up capital + reserves not exceeding Rs. 25 million

questions.text: A Small Company must not have more than how many employees at any time during the year?
The annual turnover of a Small Company should not exceed:
A company formed by splitting or reconstruction of an already existing company:
options: Does not qualify as a Small Company

questions.text: An individual is treated as a Resident Individual if present in Pakistan for at least:
Under the alternative rule, an individual is resident if present for 120 days in the current year and:
A federal government employee posted abroad during a tax year is treated as:
A company is treated as a Resident Company if it is:
For a company to be Resident, its control and management must be:
A company incorporated outside Pakistan but whose control and management is in Pakistan is:
An Association of Persons (AOP) is Resident if its control and management is:
A Non-Resident person is defined as:
options: A person living in Pakistan for 365 days
A person who is not a resident person
A person who pays tax in Pakistan

questions.text: A Resident individual is taxable in Pakistan on:
options: Pakistan source and foreign source income

questions.text: A Non-Resident person is taxable in Pakistan on:
options: Both Pakistan and foreign source income

questions.text: Which of the following is treated as a 'company' for tax purposes?
The concept of 'Taxable Income' is important because:
options: It is the base on which tax liability is calculated
It is the same as total income
It is always exempt from tax

questions.text: Penalty under the Income Tax Ordinance is generally imposed for:
Income from 'Other Sources' typically includes:
Determining whether a person is Resident or Non-Resident is important because it defines:
options: The scope of taxable income (Pakistan vs worldwide)
The color of the tax return

questions.text: The term 'Tax' under the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001 is defined in which section?
Which of the following is NOT specifically included in the definition of 'Tax' under Section 2(63)?
A person who is requested to furnish a return of total income is treated as a:
The definition of 'Person' under the Ordinance specifically includes:
options: Federal Government, foreign governments, and public international organizations

questions.text: A body corporate established under any law for the time being in force in Pakistan is treated as:
For the purposes of the Income Tax Ordinance, a non-profit charitable institution established under law is treated as:
A banking company registered under the Banking Companies Ordinance is:
options: Excluded from the definition of company
Included in the definition of company

questions.text: A cooperative society registered under the relevant law falls under the definition of:
A multinational corporation incorporated outside Pakistan but operating in Pakistan is treated as:
Which of the following is the correct list of the five heads of income under the Ordinance?
options: Salary, Business, Agriculture, Capital Gains, Other Sources
Salary, Property, Business, Capital Gains, Other Sources
Salary, Property, Profession, Capital Gains, Gifts

questions.text: Income derived from the sale of a capital asset is chargeable to tax under:
Rental income derived from letting out a house property is taxable under the head:
Dividend income received from a Pakistani company is generally taxable under:
Royalty income derived from Pakistan is classified as:
The aggregate of income computed under all five heads of income is termed as:
Taxable Income under the Ordinance is defined in:
Deductions and allowances permissible under the Ordinance are subtracted from:
The Normal Tax Year in Pakistan runs from:
A Special Tax Year is granted to a taxpayer by:
options: The Federal Board of Revenue automatically
The Federal Government on the request of the taxpayer

questions.text: Which industry is specifically cited as an example of having a Special Tax Year from 1st October to 30th September?
When a taxpayer changes from a Normal Tax Year to a Special Tax Year, the intervening gap period is known as:
During a Transitional Tax Year, the taxpayer is required to:
options: File a separate tax return for the gap months
File a combined return with the next year

questions.text: The primary reason a company or AOP requires a Principal Officer is that it is:
options: An artificial person and cannot act by itself

questions.text: The Commissioner of Income Tax may declare any person connected with the management as a Principal Officer by:
options: Issuing a notice treating him as a Principal Officer

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT explicitly mentioned in the lecture as a potential Principal Officer?
A company in which the Federal Government holds not less than 50% of the shares is classified as:
A company in which a foreign government holds 50% or more shares through a foreign investment forum is treated as:
A Unit Trust established under the Trust Act is treated as:
A company whose shares are traded on the Pakistan Stock Exchange is categorized as:
The criteria for a Small Company are primarily derived from:
For a company to qualify as a Small Company, its paid-up capital plus undistributed reserves must not exceed:
The maximum number of employees a Small Company can have at any time during the year is:
The annual turnover of a Small Company must not exceed:
A company formed by the reconstruction of an already existing large company:
options: Qualifies as a Small Company if turnover is low
Does not qualify as a Small Company

questions.text: An individual present in Pakistan for an aggregate of 183 days or more in a tax year qualifies as:
The 183-day test for individual residency counts presence in Pakistan on:
options: Only in the last six months

questions.text: Under the alternative test, an individual present for 120 days in the current year must have been present for how many days in the preceding four years to be resident?
A federal government employee posted abroad on official duty during a tax year is treated as:
For a company to be resident, its control and management must be situated:
A company incorporated outside Pakistan but having its entire control and management in Pakistan is:
A company owned by a Provincial Government of Pakistan is:
An Association of Persons is resident in Pakistan if its control and management is situated:
The key distinction between the residency rule for a Company and an AOP is that an AOP's control and management may be:
A person who does not meet the criteria of a resident person is defined as:
A resident person in Pakistan is chargeable to tax on:
options: Both Pakistan and foreign source income
Neither Pakistan nor foreign source income

questions.text: A non-resident person is chargeable to tax in Pakistan on:
The determination of residential status is critical because it defines:
options: The scope of income chargeable to tax
The due date of filing returns
The form of tax return to be used

questions.text: Foreign Source Income under the Ordinance is defined in:
A partnership firm registered under the Partnership Act, 1932 is treated as:
The broad definition of 'Person' in the Income Tax Ordinance serves to:
options: Widen the tax base by including all possible entities
